KPI001.name: % Net Promoter Score (NPS)
KPI001.description: The Net Promoter Score (NPS) measures customer loyalty and satisfaction by quantifying the willingness of customers to recommend the company's products or services. It is calculated as the percentage of promoters minus the percentage of detractors, providing a standardized metric for benchmarking customer advocacy across the organization.
KPI001.objective: Improve customer loyalty and advocacy by identifying drivers of satisfaction and addressing detractor concerns.
KPI001.input_measure: Customer survey responses categorizing respondents as promoters (9-10), passives (7-8), and detractors (0-6).
KPI001.reporting_source_detail: Customer Relationship Management system capturing sales, service, and marketing interactions.
KPI001.formula_description: (% Promoters - % Detractors) based on survey responses
KPI001.used_in_kpis: Customer satisfaction dashboards, retention strategy, and competitive benchmarking.
KPI001.comments: Industry benchmark ranges from -100 to +100. Scores above 50 are considered excellent.

KPI002.name: # Total Customer Complaints
KPI002.description: This KPI tracks the aggregate volume of customer complaints received across all channels (phone, email, web, social media), providing a comprehensive indicator of customer dissatisfaction and product/service quality issues.
KPI002.objective: Reduce customer dissatisfaction by systematically identifying, categorizing, and resolving product and service issues.
KPI002.input_measure: Customer feedback submissions received through all communication channels.
KPI002.reporting_source_detail: Customer Relationship Management system and customer service ticketing platform.
KPI002.formula_description: Sum of all complaint tickets opened in the reporting period
KPI002.used_in_kpis: Quality improvement initiatives, product development feedback, and service level agreements.
KPI002.comments: Should be analyzed by category (product, service, delivery) for actionable insights.

KPI003.name: # Justified Customer Complaints
KPI003.description: This KPI measures the subset of customer complaints that are validated as legitimate issues, distinguishing between genuine product defects or service failures and customer misunderstandings.
KPI003.objective: Identify actual operational deficiencies requiring corrective action and prioritize resources effectively.
KPI003.input_measure: Complaints validated through investigation and root cause analysis.
KPI003.reporting_source_detail: CRM system with complaint validation workflow and quality assurance review.
KPI003.formula_description: Count of complaints confirmed as valid after investigation
KPI003.used_in_kpis: Quality control metrics, supplier performance evaluation, and process improvement tracking.
KPI003.comments: Ratio of justified to total complaints indicates investigation efficiency.

KPI004.name: # Non-Justified Customer Complaints
KPI004.description: This KPI tracks customer complaints that are determined to be unsubstantiated or based on customer misunderstanding, helping assess the effectiveness of customer communication and product documentation.
KPI004.objective: Improve customer education and expectation management to reduce misunderstandings.
KPI004.input_measure: Complaints closed as invalid or due to customer misunderstanding.
KPI004.reporting_source_detail: CRM system with complaint resolution categorization.
KPI004.used_in_kpis: Customer communication effectiveness, product documentation quality.
KPI004.comments: High levels may indicate gaps in product instructions or marketing materials.

KPI005.name: # Open Customer Complaints
KPI005.description: This KPI monitors the backlog of unresolved customer complaints, measuring the responsiveness and effectiveness of the customer service organization.
KPI005.objective: Ensure timely resolution of customer issues and maintain service level commitments.
KPI005.input_measure: Complaint tickets with status 'Open' or 'In Progress' at period end.
KPI005.reporting_source_detail: CRM ticketing system with status tracking and aging reports.
KPI005.formula_description: Count of complaints not closed by period end
KPI005.used_in_kpis: Service level agreement compliance, resource planning, and customer satisfaction.
KPI005.comments: Should be monitored with aging analysis to identify stale cases.

KPI006.name: Complaints Response Time
KPI006.description: This KPI measures the average elapsed time from complaint receipt to initial response, reflecting the operational efficiency and customer-centricity of the service organization.
KPI006.objective: Minimize customer wait time and demonstrate responsiveness to build trust and satisfaction.
KPI006.input_measure: Timestamp of complaint receipt and timestamp of first response.
KPI006.reporting_source_detail: CRM system with automated timestamp tracking.
KPI006.formula_description: Average of (First Response Time - Complaint Receipt Time)
KPI006.used_in_kpis: Service level agreements, customer satisfaction score correlation.
KPI006.comments: Industry benchmark is typically within 24 hours for initial response.

KPI007.name: # Number of New Customers
KPI007.description: This KPI counts the number of first-time customers acquired within the reporting period, serving as a primary indicator of market expansion effectiveness and sales force productivity.
KPI007.objective: Accelerate customer base expansion and market penetration through effective sales and marketing strategies.
KPI007.input_measure: First purchase or contract signing records in CRM.
KPI007.reporting_source_detail: CRM system with customer acquisition date tracking.
KPI007.formula_description: Count of unique customers with first transaction in period
KPI007.used_in_kpis: Growth trajectory assessment, customer acquisition cost analysis, sales performance.
KPI007.comments: Should be analyzed alongside customer acquisition cost for efficiency metrics.

KPI008.name: % Customer Churn Rate
KPI008.description: The Customer Churn Rate measures the percentage of customers who discontinue their relationship with the company during a given period. It is a critical indicator of customer retention health, product-market fit, and competitive positioning.
KPI008.objective: Minimize customer attrition through proactive retention strategies and service quality improvements.
KPI008.input_measure: Customers at start of period, customers lost during period.
KPI008.reporting_source_detail: CRM system with customer status and contract termination tracking.
KPI008.used_in_kpis: Retention strategy effectiveness, lifetime value analysis, competitive benchmarking.
KPI008.comments: Petrochemical industry benchmark typically ranges from 5-15% annually.

KPI009.name: % Market Share
KPI009.description: This KPI quantifies the company's percentage of total sales within its defined market, providing a strategic indicator of competitive position and market penetration effectiveness.
KPI009.objective: Expand market presence and competitive position through targeted growth initiatives and customer acquisition.
KPI009.input_measure: Company sales volume and total market sales volume.
KPI009.reporting_source_detail: Market research databases and competitive intelligence platforms.
KPI009.used_in_kpis: Strategic planning, competitive analysis, and growth opportunity identification.
KPI009.comments: Should be tracked by product segment and geographic region for granularity.

KPI010.name: # Global Media Share of Voice
KPI010.description: This KPI measures the company's relative visibility in global media channels compared to competitors, quantifying brand presence and public relations effectiveness.
KPI010.objective: Enhance brand awareness and market positioning through strategic communications and media engagement.
KPI010.input_measure: Media mentions, social media engagement, and share of conversation metrics.
KPI010.reporting_source_detail: Social listening and media monitoring platforms (e.g., Meltwater, Brandwatch).
KPI010.used_in_kpis: Brand health monitoring, PR campaign effectiveness, crisis management.
KPI010.comments: Should distinguish between positive, neutral, and negative sentiment.

KPI011.name: % E-Business Penetration (Transactions)
KPI011.description: This KPI measures the percentage of total business transactions conducted through electronic channels, reflecting digital transformation progress and customer adoption of online platforms.
KPI011.objective: Drive digital transformation and self-service adoption to improve customer experience and operational efficiency.
KPI011.input_measure: Electronic transaction count and total transaction count.
KPI011.reporting_source_detail: SAP CRM and e-commerce platform transaction logs.
KPI011.used_in_kpis: Digital maturity assessment, channel strategy optimization, cost reduction tracking.
KPI011.comments: Higher penetration indicates successful transition to digital sales models.

KPI012.name: % E-Business Penetration (Volume)
KPI012.description: This KPI quantifies the proportion of total sales volume transacted through electronic business channels, measuring the digitalization of commercial operations.
KPI012.objective: Maximize digital sales volume to reduce manual processing costs and improve customer convenience.
KPI012.input_measure: Electronic sales volume (tons/USD) and total sales volume.
KPI012.reporting_source_detail: SAP CRM and e-commerce platform sales data.
KPI012.used_in_kpis: Digital sales strategy, customer self-service adoption, operational efficiency.
KPI012.comments: Volume-based metric complements transaction-based metric for complete digital picture.

KPI021.name: $ Operating Cash Flow
KPI021.description: Operating Cash Flow measures the cash generated from core business operations before financing and investing activities, providing a pure indicator of operational profitability and liquidity generation.
KPI021.objective: Ensure adequate cash generation from operations to fund growth, service debt, and maintain financial flexibility.
KPI021.input_measure: Net income, depreciation, working capital changes, and non-cash adjustments from SAP FI/CO modules.
KPI021.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP system (FI/CO modules) with automated cash flow statement generation.
KPI021.formula_description: Net Income + Depreciation & Amortization - Changes in Working Capital
KPI021.used_in_kpis: Liquidity analysis, capital allocation decisions, dividend capacity assessment.
KPI021.comments: Critical for assessing ability to fund operations without external financing.

KPI022.name: # Days of Working Capital (Period End Basis)
KPI022.description: Days of Working Capital measures the number of days required to convert working capital components (inventory, receivables, payables) into cash, reflecting the effectiveness of cash conversion cycle management.
KPI022.objective: Optimize cash conversion cycle to minimize capital tied up in operations while maintaining service levels.
KPI022.input_measure: Inventory, accounts receivable, accounts payable balances from SAP.
KPI022.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP general ledger and balance sheet modules.
KPI022.used_in_kpis: Working capital optimization, liquidity planning, operational efficiency.
KPI022.comments: Lower values indicate more efficient working capital management.

KPI023.name: # Days Sales Outstanding (DSO)
KPI023.description: Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) measures the average number of days required to collect payment after a sale has been made. It is a fundamental indicator of accounts receivable efficiency and customer creditworthiness.
KPI023.objective: Minimize collection time to improve cash flow and reduce bad debt risk.
KPI023.input_measure: Accounts receivable balance and total credit sales from SAP AR module.
KPI023.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP accounts receivable and general ledger modules.
KPI023.formula_description: (Accounts Receivable / Total Credit Sales) × Number of Days in Period
KPI023.used_in_kpis: Credit policy effectiveness, customer financial health, cash flow forecasting.
KPI023.comments: Industry benchmark typically 30-60 days for petrochemical customers.

KPI024.name: # Days Inventory Outstanding (DIO)
KPI024.description: Days Inventory Outstanding (DIO) quantifies the average duration that inventory remains on hand before conversion to sales. It measures inventory turnover efficiency and capital utilization in the supply chain.
KPI024.objective: Minimize inventory holding time to reduce carrying costs and improve capital efficiency.
KPI024.input_measure: Average inventory balance and cost of goods sold from SAP MM/PP modules.
KPI024.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP materials management and production planning modules.
KPI024.used_in_kpis: Supply chain efficiency, inventory optimization, working capital management.
KPI024.comments: Should balance with stockout risk — too low DIO may cause production disruptions.

KPI025.name: # Days Payables Outstanding (DPO)
KPI025.description: Days Payables Outstanding (DPO) measures the average time the company takes to pay its suppliers. It reflects the company's bargaining power and cash management strategy.
KPI025.objective: Optimize payment terms to preserve cash while maintaining strong supplier relationships.
KPI025.input_measure: Accounts payable balance and total purchases from SAP AP module.
KPI025.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP accounts payable and procurement modules.
KPI025.used_in_kpis: Supplier relationship management, cash flow optimization, working capital strategy.
KPI025.comments: Extended DPO improves short-term liquidity but may strain supplier relations.

KPI026.name: % Gross Margin / Sales Revenue
KPI026.description: This KPI measures gross profit as a percentage of total sales revenue, indicating the profitability of core production and sales activities before operating expenses.
KPI026.objective: Maximize gross profitability through effective pricing, cost control, and product mix optimization.
KPI026.input_measure: Revenue and cost of goods sold from SAP FI/CO profit center accounting.
KPI026.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP financial accounting and controlling modules.
KPI026.used_in_kpis: Pricing strategy, product profitability analysis, cost structure evaluation.
KPI026.comments: Petrochemical industry gross margins typically range from 15-35%.

KPI027.name: $ Gross Margin / Sales Volume
KPI027.description: This KPI calculates gross margin per unit of sales volume (metric ton), normalizing profitability by production scale to enable comparison across product lines.
KPI027.objective: Optimize unit profitability across product grades and manufacturing facilities.
KPI027.input_measure: Gross profit and total sales volume (tons) from SAP.
KPI027.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP financial and production reporting modules.
KPI027.used_in_kpis: Product mix optimization, facility performance comparison, pricing decisions.
KPI027.comments: Critical for commodity businesses where volume scale affects profitability.

KPI028.name: $ Total Fixed Cash Costs
KPI028.description: Total Fixed Cash Costs aggregate all non-variable cash expenditures required to maintain operations, including salaries, rent, utilities, and maintenance.
KPI028.objective: Control fixed cost base to improve operating leverage and break-even position.
KPI028.input_measure: General ledger accounts for fixed costs excluding depreciation.
KPI028.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP general ledger with cost center allocation.
KPI028.formula_description: Sum of all fixed cash operating expenses
KPI028.used_in_kpis: Break-even analysis, cost structure optimization, capacity planning.
KPI028.comments: Excludes non-cash items like depreciation for pure cash flow analysis.

KPI029.name: % Material Costs / Sales Revenue
KPI029.description: This KPI measures raw material and component costs as a percentage of total sales revenue, indicating the cost intensity of production and procurement efficiency.
KPI029.objective: Optimize material cost structure through procurement efficiency, supplier negotiation, and feedstock optimization.
KPI029.input_measure: Material costs and total revenue from SAP CO-PA (Profitability Analysis).
KPI029.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP controlling and profitability analysis modules.
KPI029.used_in_kpis: Procurement strategy, supplier performance, commodity price exposure management.
KPI029.comments: Petrochemical material costs typically represent 50-70% of revenue.

KPI030.name: $ Material Costs / Sales Volume
KPI030.description: This KPI normalizes material costs by production volume, calculating the per-unit raw material expenditure to enable benchmarking across product grades.
KPI030.objective: Minimize per-unit material costs through yield optimization, waste reduction, and procurement efficiency.
KPI030.input_measure: Total material costs and production volume (tons) from SAP.
KPI030.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP materials management and production planning modules.
KPI030.formula_description: Total Material Costs / Total Production Volume (tons)
KPI030.used_in_kpis: Product cost benchmarking, yield improvement tracking, procurement efficiency.
KPI030.comments: Should be tracked by feedstock type (naphtha, ethane, propane) for granularity.

KPI031.name: % G&A Costs / Sales Revenue
KPI031.description: This KPI measures general and administrative expenses as a percentage of total sales revenue, indicating the overhead burden on revenue generation.
KPI031.objective: Maintain administrative efficiency as the organization scales without proportionally increasing overhead.
KPI031.input_measure: G&A expenses and total revenue from SAP cost center accounting.
KPI031.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP cost center accounting and general ledger modules.
KPI031.used_in_kpis: Organizational efficiency, overhead control, scalability assessment.

KPI032.name: $ G&A Costs / Sales Volume
KPI032.description: This KPI calculates general and administrative costs per metric ton of sales volume, measuring the fixed overhead allocation efficiency.
KPI032.objective: Optimize administrative cost allocation across production volume to improve unit economics.
KPI032.input_measure: G&A costs and total sales volume (tons) from SAP.
KPI032.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP cost center accounting and sales reporting modules.
KPI032.used_in_kpis: Capacity planning, organizational efficiency, cost structure optimization.
KPI032.comments: Decreases with production scale due to operating leverage.

KPI033.name: % FTE Costs / Sales Revenue
KPI033.description: This KPI measures personnel costs per full-time equivalent employee relative to sales revenue, indicating labor productivity and workforce cost efficiency.
KPI033.objective: Optimize workforce productivity and compensation structure relative to revenue generation.
KPI033.input_measure: Personnel costs, FTE count, and total revenue from SAP HR and FI modules.
KPI033.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP HR (HCM) and financial accounting modules.
KPI033.used_in_kpis: Headcount planning, automation investment decisions, compensation benchmarking.
KPI033.comments: Should be analyzed alongside revenue per employee for complete productivity picture.

KPI034.name: $ Capital Expenditure (CAPEX)
KPI034.description: Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) measures the total investment in long-term assets, including property, plant, equipment, and technology infrastructure.
KPI034.objective: Allocate capital efficiently to high-return projects that support strategic growth and operational capability.
KPI034.input_measure: Capital project spending from SAP Investment Management (IM) module.
KPI034.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP Investment Management and Asset Accounting modules.
KPI034.formula_description: Sum of all capitalized asset acquisitions and construction in progress
KPI034.used_in_kpis: Strategic planning, capacity expansion tracking, ROI analysis.
KPI034.comments: Should distinguish between growth, maintenance, and regulatory compliance capex.

KPI035.name: % Total Assets / Equity
KPI035.description: This KPI measures the ratio of total assets to shareholders' equity, indicating the degree of financial leverage and asset coverage.
KPI035.objective: Maintain optimal capital structure that balances growth financing with financial risk.
KPI035.input_measure: Total assets and shareholders' equity from SAP balance sheet.
KPI035.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP financial accounting and consolidation modules.
KPI035.used_in_kpis: Capital structure analysis, solvency assessment, investor reporting.
KPI035.comments: Higher values indicate greater leverage and potentially higher financial risk.

KPI036.name: % Debt / Equity
KPI036.description: The Debt-to-Equity Ratio measures the proportion of debt financing relative to equity capital, indicating financial leverage and risk exposure.
KPI036.objective: Maintain optimal leverage that supports growth while preserving financial flexibility and credit rating.
KPI036.input_measure: Total debt and total equity from SAP balance sheet.
KPI036.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP financial accounting and treasury modules.
KPI036.used_in_kpis: Credit risk assessment, capital structure optimization, covenant compliance.
KPI036.comments: Petrochemical industry typically maintains D/E ratios between 30-80%.

KPI037.name: % Sales Revenue / Total Assets
KPI037.description: This KPI measures sales revenue generated per unit of total assets, indicating asset utilization efficiency and capital productivity.
KPI037.objective: Maximize revenue generation from the existing asset base before requiring additional capital investment.
KPI037.input_measure: Total revenue and total assets from SAP financial statements.
KPI037.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP financial accounting and asset management modules.
KPI037.used_in_kpis: Asset efficiency analysis, capital productivity, investment effectiveness.
KPI037.comments: Also known as Asset Turnover ratio. Higher values indicate better asset utilization.

KPI038.name: % Return on Equity
KPI038.description: Return on Equity (ROE) measures net income as a percentage of shareholders' equity, indicating the return generated on invested capital.
KPI038.objective: Maximize shareholder returns through profitable operations and efficient capital deployment.
KPI038.input_measure: Net income and shareholders' equity from SAP financial statements.
KPI038.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP financial accounting and consolidation modules.
KPI038.used_in_kpis: Investor reporting, performance benchmarking, capital allocation decisions.
KPI038.comments: Industry benchmark typically 10-20% for petrochemical companies.

KPI039.name: % EBIT / Sales Revenue
KPI039.description: This KPI measures earnings before interest and taxes as a percentage of sales revenue, indicating operational profitability before financing and tax considerations.
KPI039.objective: Improve core business profitability through revenue growth and operational cost discipline.
KPI039.input_measure: EBIT and total revenue from SAP profit center accounting.
KPI039.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP controlling and profitability analysis modules.
KPI039.used_in_kpis: Operational performance assessment, profitability benchmarking, margin analysis.
KPI039.comments: Focuses purely on operational performance excluding capital structure effects.

KPI040.name: % EBITDA / Sales Revenue
KPI040.description: This KPI measures earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization as a percentage of sales revenue. It indicates core operational cash generation efficiency.
KPI040.objective: Maximize operational cash generation to support reinvestment, debt service, and shareholder returns.
KPI040.input_measure: EBITDA and total revenue from SAP CO-PA and FI modules.
KPI040.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP controlling and financial accounting modules.
KPI040.used_in_kpis: Cash flow analysis, operational efficiency, capital-intensive industry benchmarking.
KPI040.comments: Critical for petrochemicals due to high depreciation from capital-intensive assets.

KPI041.name: $ EBITDA / Sales Volume
KPI041.description: This KPI calculates EBITDA per metric ton of sales volume, normalizing operational cash generation by production scale to enable comparison across facilities.
KPI041.objective: Optimize unit-level operational cash generation across manufacturing sites and product lines.
KPI041.input_measure: EBITDA and total sales volume (tons) from SAP.
KPI041.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP controlling and production reporting modules.
KPI041.used_in_kpis: Facility performance comparison, product line profitability, capacity optimization.
KPI041.comments: Higher values indicate more efficient operations or premium product positioning.

KPI042.name: % Net Income / Sales Revenue
KPI042.description: This KPI measures net income as a percentage of total sales revenue, representing the bottom-line profitability after all expenses, interest, and taxes.
KPI042.objective: Maximize overall profitability to create shareholder value and fund sustainable growth.
KPI042.input_measure: Net income and total revenue from SAP financial statements.
KPI042.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP financial accounting and consolidation modules.
KPI042.used_in_kpis: Overall profitability assessment, investor reporting, strategic performance evaluation.
KPI042.comments: Ultimate measure of financial performance. Industry benchmark typically 5-15%.

KPI043.name: % Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)
KPI043.description: Return On Capital Employed (ROCE) measures operating profit relative to the capital invested in the business, indicating the efficiency of capital deployment.
KPI043.objective: Maximize returns on invested capital to create shareholder value and fund future growth.
KPI043.input_measure: EBIT and capital employed (total assets - current liabilities) from SAP.
KPI043.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP financial accounting and consolidation modules.
KPI043.used_in_kpis: Investment decision support, business unit performance comparison, capital allocation.
KPI043.comments: Preferred metric for capital-intensive industries like petrochemicals.

KPI044.name: $ Average Selling Price (ASP)
KPI044.description: Average Selling Price (ASP) calculates the mean revenue per unit sold across all product grades and customer segments, reflecting market positioning and value capture effectiveness.
KPI044.objective: Optimize pricing strategy to maximize revenue per unit while maintaining competitive position.
KPI044.input_measure: Total revenue and total sales volume (tons) from SAP and CRM.
KPI044.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP sales and distribution (SD) module and CRM pricing data.
KPI044.used_in_kpis: Pricing strategy, product mix optimization, market segment analysis.
KPI044.comments: Should be analyzed by product grade and geographic region for strategic insights.

KPI045.name: # Sales Volume
KPI045.description: This KPI measures the total quantity of products sold in metric tons, serving as the primary volume indicator for production planning, capacity utilization, and revenue forecasting.
KPI045.objective: Maximize sales volume within capacity constraints and market demand to optimize revenue and asset utilization.
KPI045.input_measure: Delivery notes, bill of lading, and sales orders from SAP SD module.
KPI045.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP sales and distribution (SD) and materials management (MM) modules.
KPI045.formula_description: Sum of all delivered quantities in metric tons
KPI045.used_in_kpis: Production planning, capacity utilization, revenue forecasting, market share analysis.
KPI045.comments: Fundamental metric for commodity businesses. Should be tracked by product and region.

KPI048.name: $ Contribution Margin / Sales Volume
KPI048.description: This KPI calculates contribution margin per metric ton of sales volume, measuring the unit profitability after variable costs to support pricing and product mix decisions.
KPI048.objective: Optimize product mix and pricing to maximize unit contribution margin across the portfolio.
KPI048.input_measure: Revenue, variable costs, and sales volume from SAP CO-PA.
KPI048.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP controlling and profitability analysis (CO-PA) modules.
KPI048.used_in_kpis: Product mix optimization, pricing decisions, capacity allocation.
KPI048.comments: Critical for make-vs-buy and product rationalization decisions.

KPI050.name: $ G&A Costs
KPI050.description: This KPI aggregates all general and administrative expenses, including corporate overhead, finance, legal, and executive functions.
KPI050.objective: Control administrative cost base while maintaining necessary governance and support functions.
KPI050.input_measure: Cost center expenses classified as G&A from SAP CO module.
KPI050.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP cost center accounting and general ledger modules.
KPI050.formula_description: Sum of all general and administrative cost center expenses
KPI050.used_in_kpis: Cost structure analysis, organizational efficiency, overhead optimization.
KPI050.comments: Should be analyzed by function (finance, legal, HR, IT) for targeted optimization.

KPI053.name: % Contribution Margin / Sales Revenue
KPI053.description: This KPI measures contribution margin (revenue minus variable costs) as a percentage of sales revenue, indicating the proportion of revenue available to cover fixed costs and generate profit.
KPI053.objective: Maximize the portion of revenue that contributes to fixed cost coverage and profit generation.
KPI053.input_measure: Revenue, variable costs from SAP CO-PA.
KPI053.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP controlling and profitability analysis modules.
KPI053.used_in_kpis: Break-even analysis, product profitability, pricing strategy.
KPI053.comments: Higher percentages indicate greater pricing power or lower variable cost intensity.

KPI083.name: $ Material Cost
KPI083.description: This KPI aggregates total expenditures on raw materials, feedstock, and components used in production. It is a primary cost driver analysis metric.
KPI083.objective: Optimize material costs through procurement strategy, supplier negotiation, and feedstock selection.
KPI083.input_measure: Purchase orders, goods receipts, and inventory consumption from SAP MM.
KPI083.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP materials management and procurement modules.
KPI083.formula_description: Sum of all raw material and feedstock purchases
KPI083.used_in_kpis: Cost driver analysis, procurement strategy, commodity price risk management.
KPI083.comments: Should be tracked by feedstock type (naphtha, ethane, propane, butane).

KPI145.name: $ Maintenance Costs
KPI145.description: This KPI aggregates all expenditures related to equipment maintenance, repair, and preventive upkeep. It is critical for asset reliability management and total cost of ownership analysis.
KPI145.objective: Optimize maintenance spending to balance asset reliability with cost efficiency.
KPI145.input_measure: Maintenance work orders, spare parts consumption, and contractor costs from SAP PM.
KPI145.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP plant maintenance (PM) and materials management modules.
KPI145.formula_description: Sum of all maintenance-related expenditures (labor, materials, contractors)
KPI145.used_in_kpis: Asset reliability strategy, maintenance optimization, total cost of ownership.
KPI145.comments: Should distinguish between preventive, predictive, and corrective maintenance.

KPI160.name: % Working Capital / Sales Revenue
KPI160.description: This KPI measures working capital as a percentage of sales revenue, indicating the capital intensity of operations and cash conversion efficiency.
KPI160.objective: Minimize working capital requirements relative to revenue to improve cash flow and reduce financing needs.
KPI160.input_measure: Working capital (current assets - current liabilities) and revenue from SAP.
KPI160.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP financial accounting and treasury modules.
KPI160.used_in_kpis: Liquidity management, operational efficiency, financial risk assessment.
KPI160.comments: Lower values indicate more efficient working capital management.

KPI180.name: $ G&A Costs per Metric Ton Production
KPI180.description: This KPI measures general and administrative overhead costs per metric ton of production, normalizing fixed overhead by operational scale.
KPI180.objective: Optimize administrative cost efficiency as production scale increases.
KPI180.input_measure: G&A costs and total production volume from SAP.
KPI180.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP cost center accounting and production planning modules.
KPI180.used_in_kpis: Organizational efficiency, capacity planning, cost structure optimization.
KPI180.comments: Decreases with production scale due to operating leverage effect.

KPI181.name: $ Total Fixed Cash Cost per Metric Ton Production
KPI181.description: This KPI measures total fixed cash costs per metric ton of production, providing a normalized view of fixed cost burden across production scale.
KPI181.objective: Minimize fixed cost per unit through production scale and operational efficiency.
KPI181.input_measure: Fixed cash costs and production volume from SAP.
KPI181.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP cost center accounting and production planning modules.
KPI181.formula_description: Total Fixed Cash Costs / Production Volume (tons)
KPI181.used_in_kpis: Break-even analysis, capacity optimization, cost structure evaluation.
KPI181.comments: Critical for assessing operating leverage and break-even production levels.

KPI182.name: # Days To Cash (DTC)
KPI182.description: Days To Cash (DTC) measures the average time elapsed from product delivery or service completion to cash receipt. It is a critical liquidity metric.
KPI182.objective: Minimize cash collection time to improve working capital and reduce financing requirements.
KPI182.input_measure: Delivery dates and cash receipt dates from SAP AR and treasury modules.
KPI182.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP accounts receivable and treasury management modules.
KPI182.formula_description: Average of (Cash Receipt Date - Delivery Date)
KPI182.used_in_kpis: Cash flow forecasting, working capital optimization, credit policy effectiveness.
KPI182.comments: Should be tracked by customer segment and payment terms for targeted improvement.

KPI183.name: # Days Inventory On Hand (DIOH)
KPI183.description: Days Inventory On Hand (DIOH) measures the average number of days that inventory is held before being sold or consumed. It is a key indicator of inventory management efficiency.
KPI183.objective: Minimize inventory holding time while ensuring production continuity and customer service levels.
KPI183.input_measure: Average inventory balance and cost of goods sold from SAP MM.
KPI183.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP materials management and inventory management modules.
KPI183.used_in_kpis: Supply chain efficiency, inventory optimization, working capital management.
KPI183.comments: Should balance with stockout risk — too low may cause production disruptions.

KPI184.name: # Days To Payables (DTP)
KPI184.description: Days To Payables (DTP) measures the average time taken to settle obligations with suppliers. It reflects working capital management strategy.
KPI184.objective: Optimize payment timing to preserve cash while maintaining strong supplier relationships.
KPI184.input_measure: Accounts payable and total purchases from SAP AP module.
KPI184.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP accounts payable and procurement modules.
KPI184.used_in_kpis: Supplier relationship management, cash flow optimization, working capital strategy.
KPI184.comments: Extended terms improve liquidity but may affect supplier credit terms and pricing.

KPI198.name: $ Free Cash Flow (Legal Entity)
KPI198.description: This KPI measures the cash available for discretionary activities after all operating expenses and capital expenditures at the legal entity level.
KPI198.objective: Maximize discretionary cash generation to fund dividends, debt reduction, and strategic investments.
KPI198.input_measure: Operating cash flow and capital expenditures from SAP treasury and IM modules.
KPI198.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP treasury management and investment management modules.
KPI198.used_in_kpis: Dividend capacity, debt repayment planning, acquisition funding assessment.
KPI198.comments: Legal entity view is critical for tax planning and regulatory compliance.

KPI199.name: $ Free Cash Flow (SBU)
KPI199.description: This KPI measures free cash flow at the Strategic Business Unit level, enabling performance comparison across divisions.
KPI199.objective: Optimize unit-level cash generation to support resource allocation and strategic investment decisions.
KPI199.input_measure: SBU-level operating cash flow and capex from SAP CO and IM modules.
KPI199.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP controlling and investment management modules with SBU allocation.
KPI199.formula_description: SBU Operating Cash Flow - SBU Capital Expenditures
KPI199.used_in_kpis: SBU performance comparison, resource allocation, strategic investment planning.
KPI199.comments: Enables comparison across business units with different capital intensity.

KPI200.name: $ OPEX Projects
KPI200.description: This KPI tracks capital expenditures categorized as operational excellence projects, measuring investment in efficiency improvements and process optimization.
KPI200.objective: Fund operational excellence initiatives that reduce costs, improve quality, and enhance safety.
KPI200.input_measure: Capital projects classified as OPEX from SAP Investment Management.
KPI200.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP Investment Management with project classification.
KPI200.formula_description: Sum of capitalized operational excellence project spending
KPI200.used_in_kpis: Operational excellence tracking, continuous improvement investment, ROI analysis.
KPI200.comments: Should demonstrate measurable ROI through cost savings or efficiency gains.

KPI201.name: % Quarterly Price Forecasting Accuracy
KPI201.description: This KPI measures the accuracy of quarterly price predictions against actual market prices, indicating the effectiveness of market intelligence and demand forecasting.
KPI201.objective: Improve pricing forecast accuracy to support inventory planning, margin protection, and competitive positioning.
KPI201.input_measure: Forecasted prices and actual market prices from SAP and market intelligence systems.
KPI201.reporting_source_detail: SAP ERP and external market intelligence platforms (ICIS, Platts).
KPI201.used_in_kpis: Forecasting process improvement, inventory strategy, margin protection.
KPI201.comments: Higher accuracy supports better procurement timing and inventory decisions.

KPI001.name: % Net Promoter Score (NPS)
KPI001.description: The Net Promoter Score (NPS) measures customer loyalty and satisfaction by quantifying the willingness of customers to recommend the company's products or services. It is calculated as the percentage of promoters minus the percentage of detractors, providing a standardized metric for benchmarking customer advocacy across the organization.
KPI001.objective: Improve customer loyalty and advocacy by identifying drivers of satisfaction and addressing detractor concerns.
KPI001.reporting_source: Customer Relationship Management system capturing sales, service, and marketing interactions.
KPI001.input_measure: Customer survey responses categorizing respondents as promoters (9-10), passives (7-8), and detractors (0-6).
KPI001.formula_description: (% Promoters - % Detractors) based on survey responses
KPI001.used_in_kpis: Customer satisfaction dashboards, retention strategy, and competitive benchmarking.
KPI001.comments: Industry benchmark ranges from -100 to +100. Scores above 50 are considered excellent.

KPI002.name: # Total Customer Complaints
KPI002.description: This KPI tracks the aggregate volume of customer complaints received across all channels (phone, email, web, social media), providing a comprehensive indicator of customer dissatisfaction and product/service quality issues.
KPI002.objective: Reduce customer dissatisfaction by systematically identifying, categorizing, and resolving product and service issues.
KPI002.reporting_source: Customer Relationship Management system and customer service ticketing platform.
KPI002.input_measure: Customer feedback submissions received through all communication channels.
KPI002.formula_description: Sum of all complaint tickets opened in the reporting period
KPI002.used_in_kpis: Quality improvement initiatives, product development feedback, and service level agreements.
KPI002.comments: Should be analyzed by category (product, service, delivery) for actionable insights.

KPI003.name: # Justified Customer Complaints
KPI003.description: This KPI measures the subset of customer complaints that are validated as legitimate issues, distinguishing between genuine product defects or service failures and customer misunderstandings.
KPI003.objective: Identify actual operational deficiencies requiring corrective action and prioritize resources effectively.
KPI003.reporting_source: CRM system with complaint validation workflow and quality assurance review.
KPI003.input_measure: Complaints validated through investigation and root cause analysis.
KPI003.formula_description: Count of complaints confirmed as valid after investigation
KPI003.used_in_kpis: Quality control metrics, supplier performance evaluation, and process improvement tracking.
KPI003.comments: Ratio of justified to total complaints indicates investigation efficiency.

KPI004.name: # Non-Justified Customer Complaints
KPI004.description: This KPI tracks customer complaints that are determined to be unsubstantiated or based on customer misunderstanding, helping assess the effectiveness of customer communication and product documentation.
KPI004.objective: Improve customer education and expectation management to reduce misunderstandings.
KPI004.reporting_source: CRM system with complaint resolution categorization.
KPI004.input_measure: Complaints closed as invalid or due to customer misunderstanding.
KPI004.used_in_kpis: Customer communication effectiveness, product documentation quality.
KPI004.comments: High levels may indicate gaps in product instructions or marketing materials.

KPI005.name: # Open Customer Complaints
KPI005.description: This KPI monitors the backlog of unresolved customer complaints, measuring the responsiveness and effectiveness of the customer service organization.
KPI005.objective: Ensure timely resolution of customer issues and maintain service level commitments.
KPI005.reporting_source: CRM ticketing system with status tracking and aging reports.
KPI005.input_measure: Complaint tickets with status 'Open' or 'In Progress' at period end.
KPI005.formula_description: Count of complaints not closed by period end
KPI005.used_in_kpis: Service level agreement compliance, resource planning, and customer satisfaction.
KPI005.comments: Should be monitored with aging analysis to identify stale cases.

KPI006.name: Complaints Response Time (avg. days)
KPI006.description: This KPI measures the average elapsed time from complaint receipt to initial response, reflecting the operational efficiency and customer-centricity of the service organization.
KPI006.objective: Minimize customer wait time and demonstrate responsiveness to build trust and satisfaction.
KPI006.reporting_source: CRM system with automated timestamp tracking.
KPI006.input_measure: Timestamp of complaint receipt and timestamp of first response.
KPI006.formula_description: Average of (First Response Time - Complaint Receipt Time)
KPI006.used_in_kpis: Service level agreements, customer satisfaction score correlation.
KPI006.comments: Industry benchmark is typically within 24 hours for initial response.

KPI007.name: # New Customers
KPI007.description: This KPI counts the number of first-time customers acquired within the reporting period, serving as a primary indicator of market expansion effectiveness and sales force productivity.
KPI007.objective: Accelerate customer base expansion and market penetration through effective sales and marketing strategies.
KPI007.reporting_source: CRM system with customer acquisition date tracking.
KPI007.input_measure: First purchase or contract signing records in CRM.
KPI007.formula_description: Count of unique customers with first transaction in period
KPI007.used_in_kpis: Growth trajectory assessment, customer acquisition cost analysis, sales performance.
KPI007.comments: Should be analyzed alongside customer acquisition cost for efficiency metrics.

KPI008.name: % Customer Churn Rate
KPI008.description: The Customer Churn Rate measures the percentage of customers who discontinue their relationship with the company during a given period. It is a critical indicator of customer retention health, product-market fit, and competitive positioning.
KPI008.objective: Minimize customer attrition through proactive retention strategies and service quality improvements.
KPI008.reporting_source: CRM system with customer status and contract termination tracking.
KPI008.input_measure: Customers at start of period, customers lost during period.
KPI008.used_in_kpis: Retention strategy effectiveness, lifetime value analysis, competitive benchmarking.
KPI008.comments: Petrochemical industry benchmark typically ranges from 5-15% annually.

KPI021.name: $ Operating Cash Flow
KPI021.description: Operating Cash Flow measures the cash generated from core business operations before financing and investing activities, providing a pure indicator of operational profitability and liquidity generation.
KPI021.objective: Ensure adequate cash generation from operations to fund growth, service debt, and maintain financial flexibility.
KPI021.reporting_source: SAP ERP system (FI/CO modules) with automated cash flow statement generation.
KPI021.input_measure: Net income, depreciation, working capital changes, and non-cash adjustments from SAP FI/CO modules.
KPI021.formula_description: Net Income + Depreciation & Amortization - Changes in Working Capital
KPI021.used_in_kpis: Liquidity analysis, capital allocation decisions, dividend capacity assessment.
KPI021.comments: Critical for assessing ability to fund operations without external financing.

KPI022.name: # Days of Working Capital
KPI022.description: Days of Working Capital measures the number of days required to convert working capital components (inventory, receivables, payables) into cash, reflecting the effectiveness of cash conversion cycle management.
KPI022.objective: Optimize cash conversion cycle to minimize capital tied up in operations while maintaining service levels.
KPI022.reporting_source: SAP ERP general ledger and balance sheet modules.
KPI022.input_measure: Inventory, accounts receivable, accounts payable balances from SAP.
KPI022.used_in_kpis: Working capital optimization, liquidity planning, operational efficiency.
KPI022.comments: Lower values indicate more efficient working capital management.

KPI066.name: $ Variable Cost / Total Production
KPI066.description: This KPI calculates the variable cost per unit of total production, measuring the efficiency of variable cost management and its impact on overall production costs.
KPI066.objective: Optimize variable costs to improve production profitability and competitiveness.
KPI066.reporting_source: SAP ERP cost accounting and production planning modules.
KPI066.input_measure: Total variable costs and total production volume from SAP.
KPI066.used_in_kpis: Cost structure analysis, operational efficiency, product profitability.
KPI066.comments: Lower values indicate better variable cost control.

KPI067.name: $ Fixed Cash Cost / Total Production
KPI067.description: This KPI measures the fixed cash cost per unit of total production, providing insight into the fixed cost burden on production and scalability.
KPI067.objective: Optimize fixed cash costs to improve production efficiency and break-even position.
KPI067.reporting_source: SAP ERP cost accounting and production planning modules.
KPI067.input_measure: Total fixed cash costs and total production volume from SAP.
KPI067.used_in_kpis: Break-even analysis, capacity utilization, cost structure optimization.
KPI067.comments: Lower values indicate better fixed cost management.

KPI069.name: % Service Factor
KPI069.description: The Service Factor measures the percentage of on-time order delivery, reflecting supply chain reliability and customer service quality.
KPI069.objective: Maximize on-time delivery performance to improve customer satisfaction and reduce expediting costs.
KPI069.reporting_source: Order management system with delivery tracking.
KPI069.input_measure: On-time orders and total orders in the period.
KPI069.used_in_kpis: Supply chain efficiency, customer satisfaction, operational excellence.
KPI069.comments: Industry benchmark is typically above 95% for high-performing organizations.

KPI070.name: % Capacity Utilization
KPI070.description: This KPI measures the actual output as a percentage of design capacity, indicating how effectively the production capacity is being utilized.
KPI070.objective: Maximize capacity utilization to reduce unit costs and improve asset efficiency.
KPI070.reporting_source: SAP ERP production planning and manufacturing execution modules.
KPI070.input_measure: Actual output and design capacity from SAP.
KPI070.used_in_kpis: Capital planning, operational performance, capacity management.
KPI070.comments: High utilization rates indicate efficient use of assets but may signal upcoming capacity constraints.

KPI071.name: % Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE)
KPI071.description: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) combines Availability, Performance, and Quality into a single metric that measures the overall effectiveness of manufacturing equipment.
KPI071.objective: Maximize OEE by reducing downtime, speed losses, and quality defects.
KPI071.reporting_source: MES/Production systems and equipment runtime counters.
KPI071.input_measure: Availability, performance, and quality factors calculated for the asset or line.
KPI071.used_in_kpis: Production efficiency, maintenance effectiveness, lean manufacturing.

KPI077.name: % On-Stream Factor
KPI077.description: The On-Stream Factor measures the percentage of time a plant or equipment is in operation relative to total available time, reflecting operational reliability.
KPI077.objective: Maximize on-stream time to increase production throughput and asset productivity.
KPI077.reporting_source: SAP ERP plant maintenance and operations modules.
KPI077.input_measure: Operating hours and total available hours.
KPI077.used_in_kpis: Plant reliability, maintenance optimization, production planning.
KPI077.comments: High on-stream factor indicates reliable operations and effective maintenance.

KPI084.name: $ Manufacturing Fixed Cash Cost Normalized (to TA)
KPI084.description: This KPI normalizes fixed manufacturing cash costs by turnaround events, measuring cost efficiency during maintenance cycles.
KPI084.objective: Optimize fixed cost allocation across turnaround schedules and improve cost management.
KPI084.reporting_source: SAP ERP plant maintenance and financial accounting modules.
KPI084.input_measure: Fixed cash costs and turnaround event counts.
KPI084.formula_description: (Total Fixed Cash Costs) / (Number of Turnaround Events)
KPI084.used_in_kpis: Turnaround cost management, fixed cost optimization, maintenance planning.
KPI084.comments: Lower normalized costs indicate better fixed cost management per turnaround.

KPI100.name: # Days of Working Inventory
KPI100.description: Days of Working Inventory measures the average number of days inventory is held before being consumed or sold, indicating inventory efficiency.
KPI100.objective: Minimize inventory holding days while ensuring uninterrupted production.
KPI100.reporting_source: SAP ERP materials management and production modules.
KPI100.input_measure: Inventory balance and daily cost of goods sold.
KPI100.used_in_kpis: Inventory optimization, working capital management, supply chain efficiency.
KPI100.comments: Should balance with stockout risk – too low may cause production disruptions.

KPI160.name: % Working Capital / Sales Revenue
KPI160.description: This KPI measures working capital as a percentage of sales revenue, indicating the capital intensity of operations and cash conversion efficiency.
KPI160.objective: Minimize working capital requirements relative to revenue to improve cash flow and reduce financing needs.
KPI160.reporting_source: SAP ERP financial accounting and treasury modules.
KPI160.input_measure: Working capital (current assets - current liabilities) and revenue from SAP.
KPI160.used_in_kpis: Liquidity management, operational efficiency, financial risk assessment.
KPI160.comments: Lower values indicate more efficient working capital management.

KPI169.name: % Emergency Maintenance Cost
KPI169.description: This KPI measures the percentage of total maintenance costs spent on emergency maintenance, indicating the effectiveness of preventive and predictive maintenance programs.
KPI169.objective: Reduce emergency maintenance costs through proactive reliability improvements.
KPI169.reporting_source: SAP ERP plant maintenance and work order modules.
KPI169.input_measure: Emergency maintenance costs and total maintenance costs.
KPI169.used_in_kpis: Maintenance strategy effectiveness, asset reliability, cost optimization.
KPI169.comments: Lower percentages indicate better preventive maintenance practices.

KPI176.name: % Maintenance Man Hours Estimation Accuracy
KPI176.description: This KPI measures the accuracy of maintenance man-hour estimates by comparing estimated hours to actual hours worked.
KPI176.objective: Improve maintenance planning accuracy and resource allocation.
KPI176.reporting_source: SAP ERP plant maintenance and work order modules.
KPI176.input_measure: Estimated hours and actual hours from maintenance work orders.
KPI176.used_in_kpis: Maintenance planning effectiveness, resource utilization, cost estimation.
KPI176.comments: Values close to 100% indicate accurate estimation.

KPI182.name: # Days To Cash (DTC)
KPI182.description: Days To Cash (DTC) measures the average time elapsed from product delivery or service completion to cash receipt. It is a critical liquidity metric.
KPI182.objective: Minimize cash collection time to improve working capital and reduce financing requirements.
KPI182.reporting_source: SAP ERP accounts receivable and treasury management modules.
KPI182.input_measure: Delivery dates and cash receipt dates from SAP AR and treasury modules.
KPI182.formula_description: Average of (Cash Receipt Date - Delivery Date)
KPI182.used_in_kpis: Cash flow forecasting, working capital optimization, credit policy effectiveness.
KPI182.comments: Should be tracked by customer segment and payment terms for targeted improvement.

KPI183.name: # Days Inventory On Hand (DIOH)
KPI183.description: Days Inventory On Hand (DIOH) measures the average number of days that inventory is held before being sold or consumed. It is a key indicator of inventory management efficiency.
KPI183.objective: Minimize inventory holding time while ensuring production continuity and customer service levels.
KPI183.reporting_source: SAP ERP materials management and inventory management modules.
KPI183.input_measure: Average inventory balance and cost of goods sold from SAP MM.
KPI183.used_in_kpis: Supply chain efficiency, inventory optimization, working capital management.
KPI183.comments: Should balance with stockout risk — too low may cause production disruptions.

KPI184.name: # Days To Payables (DTP)
KPI184.description: Days To Payables (DTP) measures the average time taken to settle obligations with suppliers. It reflects working capital management strategy.
KPI184.objective: Optimize payment timing to preserve cash while maintaining strong supplier relationships.
KPI184.reporting_source: SAP ERP accounts payable and procurement modules.
KPI184.input_measure: Accounts payable and total purchases from SAP AP module.
KPI184.used_in_kpis: Supplier relationship management, cash flow optimization, working capital strategy.
KPI184.comments: Extended terms improve liquidity but may affect supplier credit terms and pricing.

KPI213.name: % Benchmark Capability Utilization
KPI213.description: This KPI compares actual output to a benchmark capacity, measuring how well the plant performs relative to industry or internal standards.
KPI213.objective: Identify gaps between actual and benchmark performance to drive improvements.
KPI213.reporting_source: SAP ERP production planning and benchmarking tools.
KPI213.used_in_kpis: Performance benchmarking, competitive analysis, capability improvement.
KPI213.comments: Lower than 100% indicates underperformance relative to benchmark.

Distillation Column Efficiency %.name: Distillation Column Efficiency %
Distillation Column Efficiency %.description: This KPI measures the efficiency of distillation column operations by comparing theoretical separation stages to actual stages achieved, indicating column performance.
Distillation Column Efficiency %.objective: Optimize distillation column operation to maximize separation efficiency and reduce energy consumption.
Distillation Column Efficiency %.reporting_source: Process historian and DCS (Distributed Control System) data.
Distillation Column Efficiency %.input_measure: Theoretical stages and actual stages from process data.
Distillation Column Efficiency %.used_in_kpis: Process optimization, energy efficiency, product quality.
Distillation Column Efficiency %.comments: Higher efficiency indicates better column performance.

Furnace Efficiency %.name: Furnace Efficiency %
Furnace Efficiency %.description: This KPI measures the energy efficiency of furnaces by comparing useful energy output to total energy input.
Furnace Efficiency %.objective: Maximize furnace thermal efficiency to reduce fuel consumption and emissions.
Furnace Efficiency %.input_measure: Useful energy output and total energy input.
Furnace Efficiency %.used_in_kpis: Energy management, emission reduction, operating cost control.
Furnace Efficiency %.comments: Higher efficiency indicates better energy utilization.

Steam-to-Ethylene Ratio.name: Steam-to-Ethylene Ratio
Steam-to-Ethylene Ratio.description: This KPI measures the ratio of steam to ethylene in the cracking furnace, indicating operating efficiency and feed quality.
Steam-to-Ethylene Ratio.objective: Optimize steam-to-ethylene ratio to maximize product yield and reduce energy consumption.
Steam-to-Ethylene Ratio.reporting_source: Process historian with flow meter data.
Steam-to-Ethylene Ratio.input_measure: Steam flow rate and ethylene production rate.
Steam-to-Ethylene Ratio.used_in_kpis: Cracking furnace optimization, energy efficiency, product yield.
Steam-to-Ethylene Ratio.comments: Lower ratios indicate better efficiency but must be balanced with reaction requirements.

Catalyst Activity Index.name: Catalyst Activity Index
Catalyst Activity Index.description: This KPI measures the activity level of catalysts used in reactions, indicating the remaining useful life and effectiveness of the catalyst.
Catalyst Activity Index.objective: Monitor catalyst health to optimize replacement timing and reaction performance.
Catalyst Activity Index.reporting_source: Laboratory analysis and process historian data.
Catalyst Activity Index.input_measure: Active sites and total sites from catalyst characterization.
Catalyst Activity Index.used_in_kpis: Reaction efficiency, catalyst life management, cost optimization.
Catalyst Activity Index.comments: Lower index indicates deactivation and need for regeneration or replacement.

Compressor Availability %.name: Compressor Availability %
Compressor Availability %.description: This KPI measures the percentage of time compressors are available for operation, considering planned and unplanned downtime.
Compressor Availability %.objective: Maximize compressor availability to ensure continuous production and reliability.
Compressor Availability %.reporting_source: Process historian and maintenance management system.
Compressor Availability %.used_in_kpis: Asset reliability, maintenance effectiveness, production continuity.
Compressor Availability %.comments: High availability is critical for continuous process plants.

ProductionLoss.name: # Production Loss
ProductionLoss.description: This KPI measures the total production loss, calculated as the difference between planned output and actual output.
ProductionLoss.objective: Identify and eliminate sources of production loss to maximize output and reduce waste.
ProductionLoss.reporting_source: SAP ERP production planning and execution modules.
ProductionLoss.input_measure: Planned output and actual output from production orders.
ProductionLoss.used_in_kpis: Operational efficiency, yield improvement, waste reduction.
ProductionLoss.comments: Lower production loss indicates better operational performance.

Reactor Conversion %.name: Reactor Conversion %
Reactor Conversion %.description: This KPI measures the percentage of reactants converted into products in a chemical reactor, indicating reaction efficiency.
Reactor Conversion %.objective: Maximize conversion to increase yield and reduce raw material consumption.
Reactor Conversion %.used_in_kpis: Reaction optimization, yield improvement, raw material efficiency.
Reactor Conversion %.comments: Higher conversion indicates more efficient reaction.

Reactor Selectivity %.name: Reactor Selectivity %
Reactor Selectivity %.description: This KPI measures the selectivity of a chemical reaction, i.e., the proportion of desired product relative to total products formed.
Reactor Selectivity %.objective: Improve selectivity to maximize valuable product yield and reduce byproducts.
Reactor Selectivity %.input_measure: Desired product amount and total products formed.
Reactor Selectivity %.used_in_kpis: Reaction optimization, product quality, byproduct reduction.
Reactor Selectivity %.comments: Higher selectivity indicates more efficient reaction pathway.

Polymerization Conversion %.name: Polymerization Conversion %
Polymerization Conversion %.description: This KPI measures the conversion efficiency of polymerization reactions, indicating the effectiveness of the polymerization process.
Polymerization Conversion %.objective: Maximize conversion to increase polymer yield and reduce monomer waste.
Polymerization Conversion %.input_measure: Converted monomer mass and feed monomer mass.
Polymerization Conversion %.used_in_kpis: Polymer production efficiency, quality control, raw material utilization.
Polymerization Conversion %.comments: Higher conversion indicates better polymerization performance.

Dehydrogenation Selectivity %.name: Dehydrogenation Selectivity %
Dehydrogenation Selectivity %.description: This KPI measures the selectivity of dehydrogenation reactions, i.e., the proportion of desired dehydrogenated product relative to total products.
Dehydrogenation Selectivity %.objective: Improve selectivity to maximize desired product yield and reduce side reactions.
Dehydrogenation Selectivity %.used_in_kpis: Reaction optimization, product quality, byproduct minimization.
Dehydrogenation Selectivity %.comments: Higher selectivity indicates more efficient dehydrogenation.

Hydrogen Utilization %.name: Hydrogen Utilization %
Hydrogen Utilization %.description: This KPI measures the efficiency of hydrogen usage in hydrogen-consuming processes, comparing consumed hydrogen to supplied hydrogen.
Hydrogen Utilization %.objective: Maximize hydrogen utilization to reduce consumption and improve process efficiency.
Hydrogen Utilization %.used_in_kpis: Hydrogen management, cost optimization, energy efficiency.
Hydrogen Utilization %.comments: Higher utilization indicates better hydrogen efficiency.

Aromatics Recovery %.name: Aromatics Recovery %
Aromatics Recovery %.description: This KPI measures the recovery efficiency of aromatics from feed streams, indicating the effectiveness of the separation process.
Aromatics Recovery %.objective: Maximize aromatics recovery to increase valuable product yield.
Aromatics Recovery %.reporting_source: Process historian and quality control data.
Aromatics Recovery %.input_measure: Recovered aromatics and feed aromatics content.
Aromatics Recovery %.used_in_kpis: Separation efficiency, product yield, operational performance.
Aromatics Recovery %.comments: Higher recovery indicates better separation performance.

Flare Reduction %.name: Flare Reduction %
Flare Reduction %.description: This KPI measures the percentage reduction in flaring volume relative to total gas volume, indicating progress in flare minimization.
Flare Reduction %.objective: Reduce flaring to lower emissions and improve environmental performance.
Flare Reduction %.reporting_source: Process historian and environmental monitoring systems.
Flare Reduction %.input_measure: Flare volume and total gas volume.
Flare Reduction %.comments: Higher reduction indicates better flare minimization performance.

Benzene Purity %.name: Benzene Purity %
Benzene Purity %.description: This KPI measures the purity of benzene product, indicating the quality of the distillation and refining process.
Benzene Purity %.objective: Maintain high benzene purity to meet product specifications and customer requirements.
Benzene Purity %.reporting_source: Laboratory analysis and quality control system.
Benzene Purity %.input_measure: Benzene content measured by analytical methods.
Benzene Purity %.formula_description: Measured purity from GC or other analytical techniques
Benzene Purity %.used_in_kpis: Product quality, process control, customer satisfaction.
Benzene Purity %.comments: Typical specification is >99.5% for industrial benzene.

Ethylene Yield %.name: Ethylene Yield %
Ethylene Yield %.description: This KPI measures the yield of ethylene produced from feedstock, indicating the efficiency of the cracking process.
Ethylene Yield %.objective: Maximize ethylene yield to improve profitability and resource efficiency.
Ethylene Yield %.used_in_kpis: Cracking efficiency, feedstock utilization, operational performance.
Ethylene Yield %.comments: Yield depends on feedstock quality and operating conditions.

Propylene Yield %.name: Propylene Yield %
Propylene Yield %.description: This KPI measures the yield of propylene produced from feedstock, indicating the efficiency of the cracking process.
Propylene Yield %.objective: Maximize propylene yield to improve profitability and resource efficiency.
Propylene Yield %.used_in_kpis: Cracking efficiency, feedstock utilization, operational performance.
Propylene Yield %.comments: Yield depends on feedstock quality and operating conditions.

Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio.name: Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio
Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio.description: The ratio of propylene to ethylene production volumes, assessing product slate balance.
Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio.objective: Align production strategy with market demand and margins.
Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio.reporting_source: Process historian (e.g., AVEVA PI System) and control system archives.
Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio.input_measure: Total propylene and ethylene produced in the measurement period.
Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio.used_in_kpis: Production planning, product mix optimization, market alignment.
Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio.comments: Optimal ratio varies based on market prices.

kpi flare recovery pct.name: kpi flare recovery pct
kpi flare recovery pct.description: Share of total gas recovered instead of flared, indicating recovery system effectiveness.
kpi flare recovery pct.objective: Reduce flaring and emissions by improving recovery system reliability.
kpi flare recovery pct.reporting_source: Process historian (flare meters and recovery system flow meters).
kpi flare recovery pct.input_measure: Gas flared volume and gas recovered volume over the measurement period.
kpi flare recovery pct.comments: Higher recovery indicates better environmental performance.

OEE %.name: OEE %
OEE %.description: Overall Equipment Effectiveness combines Availability, Performance, and Quality into a single effectiveness score.
OEE %.objective: Increase effective productive time by reducing downtime, speed losses, and quality losses.
OEE %.reporting_source: MES/Production systems and equipment runtime counters.
OEE %.input_measure: Availability, performance, and quality factors calculated for the asset or line.
OEE %.used_in_kpis: Production efficiency, maintenance effectiveness, lean manufacturing.

Furnace OEE %.name: Furnace OEE %
Furnace OEE %.description: Furnace Overall Equipment Effectiveness combines Availability, Performance, and Quality specific to furnace operations, measuring furnace effectiveness.
Furnace OEE %.objective: Increase furnace effective productive time by reducing downtime, speed losses, and quality losses.
Furnace OEE %.reporting_source: MES/Production systems and furnace runtime counters.
Furnace OEE %.input_measure: Availability, performance, and quality factors calculated for the furnace.
Furnace OEE %.used_in_kpis: Furnace efficiency, production optimization, energy management.
Furnace OEE %.comments: Furnace OEE is critical for energy-intensive processes.

Cooling Water Delta-T (°C.name: Cooling Water Delta-T (°C)
Cooling Water Delta-T (°C.description: This KPI measures the temperature difference between cooling water inlet and outlet, indicating heat transfer effectiveness in cooling systems.
Cooling Water Delta-T (°C.objective: Optimize cooling water flow and heat exchange to ensure adequate cooling and energy efficiency.
Cooling Water Delta-T (°C.input_measure: Inlet temperature and outlet temperature of cooling water.
Cooling Water Delta-T (°C.used_in_kpis: Heat exchanger performance, cooling system optimization, energy management.
Cooling Water Delta-T (°C.comments: Higher delta-T indicates better heat transfer but may increase fouling risk.

Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio.name: Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio
Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio.description: This KPI measures the ratio of maintenance costs to total operating costs, indicating the relative investment in asset maintenance.
Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio.objective: Optimize maintenance spending to balance asset reliability and operating cost efficiency.
Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio.reporting_source: SAP ERP cost center accounting and maintenance modules.
Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio.input_measure: Total maintenance costs and total operating costs.
Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio.used_in_kpis: Maintenance effectiveness, cost structure, asset reliability.
Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio.comments: Benchmark varies by industry but typically 10-20% in petrochemicals.

Energy Intensity (BTU/lb).name: Energy Intensity (BTU/lb)
Energy Intensity (BTU/lb).description: Measures the energy consumed per unit of production, indicating operational efficiency and sustainability.
Energy Intensity (BTU/lb).objective: Reduce energy consumption per unit to lower costs and environmental impact.
Energy Intensity (BTU/lb).reporting_source: Energy management systems and production records.
Energy Intensity (BTU/lb).input_measure: Total energy consumed (BTU) and total production (lbs).
Energy Intensity (BTU/lb).used_in_kpis: Sustainability reporting, operational efficiency, cost reduction.
Energy Intensity (BTU/lb).comments: Lower values indicate better energy efficiency.

Carbon Footprint (kg CO2/ton).name: Carbon Footprint (kg CO2/ton)
Carbon Footprint (kg CO2/ton).description: Measures greenhouse gas emissions per unit of production, reflecting environmental impact.
Carbon Footprint (kg CO2/ton).objective: Minimize carbon footprint to meet regulatory and sustainability goals.
Carbon Footprint (kg CO2/ton).reporting_source: Environmental monitoring systems and emissions data.
Carbon Footprint (kg CO2/ton).input_measure: Total CO2 emissions (kg) and total production (tons).
Carbon Footprint (kg CO2/ton).used_in_kpis: Environmental compliance, sustainability reporting, carbon trading.
Carbon Footprint (kg CO2/ton).comments: Lower values indicate better environmental performance.

Water Consumption (gallons/ton).name: Water Consumption (gallons/ton)
Water Consumption (gallons/ton).description: Measures water usage per unit of production, indicating water efficiency.
Water Consumption (gallons/ton).objective: Reduce water consumption to conserve resources and lower costs.
Water Consumption (gallons/ton).input_measure: Total water used (gallons) and total production (tons).
Water Consumption (gallons/ton).comments: Lower values indicate better water efficiency.

Asset Utilization (%).name: Asset Utilization (%)
Asset Utilization (%).description: Measures the percentage of asset capacity actually used, reflecting capital efficiency.
Asset Utilization (%).objective: Maximize asset utilization to improve return on assets.
Asset Utilization (%).used_in_kpis: Capital efficiency, capacity planning, operational performance.
Asset Utilization (%).comments: Higher values indicate better asset utilization.

Overall Labor Effectiveness (OLE) (%).name: Overall Labor Effectiveness (OLE) (%)
Overall Labor Effectiveness (OLE) (%).description: Measures labor productivity by combining availability, performance, and quality of the workforce.
Overall Labor Effectiveness (OLE) (%).objective: Increase labor effectiveness to reduce costs and improve throughput.
Overall Labor Effectiveness (OLE) (%).input_measure: Available hours, productive hours, and quality output.
Overall Labor Effectiveness (OLE) (%).used_in_kpis: Workforce productivity, staffing optimization, cost management.

First Pass Yield (%).name: First Pass Yield (%)
First Pass Yield (%).description: Measures the percentage of products that meet quality standards without rework, indicating process quality.
First Pass Yield (%).objective: Increase first pass yield to reduce rework and waste.
First Pass Yield (%).input_measure: Good units and total units produced.
First Pass Yield (%).used_in_kpis: Quality improvement, cost reduction, process capability.
First Pass Yield (%).comments: Higher values indicate better process quality.

Schedule Adherence (%).name: Schedule Adherence (%)
Schedule Adherence (%).description: Measures how closely actual production matches the planned schedule.
Schedule Adherence (%).objective: Improve schedule adherence to optimize resource utilization and meet customer commitments.
Schedule Adherence (%).comments: Values close to 100% indicate excellent planning.

Inventory Turnover (times/year).name: Inventory Turnover (times/year)
Inventory Turnover (times/year).description: Measures how many times inventory is sold and replaced over a period, reflecting inventory management efficiency.
Inventory Turnover (times/year).objective: Optimize inventory turnover to reduce holding costs and improve cash flow.
Inventory Turnover (times/year).input_measure: Cost of goods sold and average inventory.
Inventory Turnover (times/year).used_in_kpis: Inventory management, working capital optimization, supply chain efficiency.
Inventory Turnover (times/year).comments: Higher turnover indicates better inventory management.

Supplier On-Time Delivery (%).name: Supplier On-Time Delivery (%)
Supplier On-Time Delivery (%).description: Measures the percentage of supplier deliveries that arrive on time, reflecting supply chain reliability.
Supplier On-Time Delivery (%).objective: Increase supplier on-time delivery to ensure production continuity.
Supplier On-Time Delivery (%).used_in_kpis: Supply chain reliability, procurement performance, production planning.

Safety Incident Rate (per 200,000 hours).name: Safety Incident Rate (per 200,000 hours)
Safety Incident Rate (per 200,000 hours).description: Measures the number of safety incidents per 200,000 hours worked, standard for OSHA reporting.
Safety Incident Rate (per 200,000 hours).objective: Reduce incidents to improve workplace safety and reduce costs.
Safety Incident Rate (per 200,000 hours).input_measure: Number of incidents and total hours worked.
Safety Incident Rate (per 200,000 hours).comments: Lower values indicate better safety performance.

Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) (hours).name: Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) (hours)
Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) (hours).description: Measures the average time required to repair equipment after a failure.
Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) (hours).objective: Reduce MTTR to minimize downtime and improve asset availability.
Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) (hours).reporting_source: Maintenance work orders and downtime logs.
Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) (hours).input_measure: Total repair time and number of repairs.
Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) (hours).formula_description: Total Repair Time / Number of Repairs
Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) (hours).used_in_kpis: Maintenance effectiveness, asset reliability, downtime reduction.
Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) (hours).comments: Lower values indicate faster repair capabilities.

Process Capability Index (Cpk).name: Process Capability Index (Cpk)
Process Capability Index (Cpk).description: Measures the ability of a process to produce output within specification limits, reflecting process stability.
Process Capability Index (Cpk).objective: Improve Cpk to reduce variability and defects.
Process Capability Index (Cpk).reporting_source: Quality control charts and statistical analysis.
Process Capability Index (Cpk).input_measure: USL, LSL, mean, and standard deviation.
Process Capability Index (Cpk).used_in_kpis: Quality improvement, process control, Six Sigma.

Overall Cycle Time (hours).name: Overall Cycle Time (hours)
Overall Cycle Time (hours).description: Measures the total time required to process a unit from start to finish, reflecting production speed.
Overall Cycle Time (hours).objective: Reduce cycle time to increase throughput and agility.
Overall Cycle Time (hours).input_measure: Total process time and number of units.
Overall Cycle Time (hours).formula_description: Total Process Time / Number of Units
Overall Cycle Time (hours).used_in_kpis: Operational efficiency, throughput, lead time reduction.

Downtime Percentage (%).name: Downtime Percentage (%)
Downtime Percentage (%).description: Measures the percentage of time equipment is not available for production due to unplanned downtime.
Downtime Percentage (%).objective: Reduce downtime to improve asset availability and output.
Downtime Percentage (%).input_measure: Downtime hours and total available hours.

Reliability (%).name: Reliability (%)
Reliability (%).description: Measures the probability that equipment will operate without failure over a given period.
Reliability (%).objective: Increase reliability to reduce failures and improve uptime.
Reliability (%).comments: Higher values indicate more reliable equipment.

Quality Yield (%).name: Quality Yield (%)
Quality Yield (%).description: Measures the proportion of output that meets quality standards, reflecting process effectiveness.
Quality Yield (%).objective: Increase quality yield to reduce waste and improve customer satisfaction.
Quality Yield (%).used_in_kpis: Quality management, waste reduction, Six Sigma.
Quality Yield (%).comments: Higher values indicate better quality performance.

Scrap Rate (%).name: Scrap Rate (%)
Scrap Rate (%).description: Measures the percentage of material that becomes waste during production.
Scrap Rate (%).objective: Reduce scrap rate to minimize waste and material costs.
Scrap Rate (%).input_measure: Scrap material and total material used.
Scrap Rate (%).comments: Lower values indicate better material efficiency.

Overall Vibration (mm/s).name: Overall Vibration (mm/s)
Overall Vibration (mm/s).description: Measures the overall vibration level of rotating equipment, indicating mechanical health.
Overall Vibration (mm/s).objective: Monitor vibration to predict failures and schedule maintenance.
Overall Vibration (mm/s).used_in_kpis: Condition monitoring, predictive maintenance, asset health.

Lubricant Oil Analysis (particle count).name: Lubricant Oil Analysis (particle count)
Lubricant Oil Analysis (particle count).description: Measures the number of particles in lubricating oil, indicating wear and contamination.
Lubricant Oil Analysis (particle count).objective: Track oil quality to extend equipment life and prevent failures.
Lubricant Oil Analysis (particle count).used_in_kpis: Predictive maintenance, asset reliability, cost optimization.
Lubricant Oil Analysis (particle count).comments: High counts indicate excessive wear or contamination.
